L1.area: Multi-tenant data access control and query permission enforcement

L2.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists where a client-controlled HTTP request header causes the server to skip internal permission checks and disable tenant-level data isolation on database queries. A low-privileged authenticated user can send a forged header to access data belonging to other tenants, read sensitive user credential fields through nested model relations, and achieve full account takeover by leaking plaintext password reset tokens.

L3.area: Tenant permission scoping logic in the database and analytics database permission layers
L3.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists where a client-controlled HTTP request header that signals a cross-tenant query causes the server to bypass tenant-level data isolation. A conditional logic gap in the tenant scoping code means that when both a tenant identifier and the cross-tenant flag are present, no branch of the scoping logic applies, leaving queries completely unfiltered. A low-privileged authenticated user can exploit this gap to access data belonging to other tenants, read sensitive user credential fields through nested model relations, and achieve full account takeover by leaking plaintext password reset tokens.

ground_truth.reason: The tenant permission scoping logic fails to apply any tenant filter when a request carries both a tenant identifier and a client-controlled cross-tenant query flag, allowing a low-privileged user to bypass authorization and access all tenants' data.
